patient.instructions:

Start by volunteering: “I can’t catch my breath and it’s getting worse fast,” plus dry cough a few days, wiped out/weak, low fever/chills, and getting winded walking to the bathroom. Emphasize it’s worsening quickly.

If asked, add: it worsened over 2–3 days after a week of bad-cold symptoms; chest tightness on deep breaths; fast breathing, can’t get a full breath; waking at night short of breath; muscle aches/headaches; minimal clear sputum; no blood; lightheaded standing; poor appetite.

Express misconceptions: worry it’s your heart/pulmonary hypertension; mention ankle swelling and past “walking pneumonia,” assume bacterial.

If clinician shows empathy, soften slightly but still worry about job loss and costs; minimize symptoms to avoid tests/hospitalization.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as "acute interstitial pneumonia" and links it to "respiratory failure" when explaining findings and urgency.
